result.title: Urban Flood Vulnerability and Inundation Dynamics in Bangalore — A Technical Study
result.agency: Indian Institute of Science (IISc) — Centre for Infrastructure, Sustainable Transportation and Urban Planning (CiSTUP)
result.summary: Technical research study examining urban flood vulnerability in Bangalore with empirical analysis of flood events from 2015–2022. Establishes rainfall-inundation correlation curves for major wards, analyses lake backflow contribution to road inundation, provides corridor-level delay multipliers for logistics planning, and recommends ward-level early warning thresholds. Calibration reference for the UrbanClimate AI flood and logistics agents.

table_of_contents.title: Study Scope, Methodology, and Ward Coverage
table_of_contents.summary: Overview of study design, data sources used, spatial coverage, and field survey methodology. Covers 10 high-risk wards with documented flood history from 2015–2022. Establishes the analytical framework used in subsequent sections.

table_of_contents.title: Rainfall-Inundation Correlation Findings by Ward
table_of_contents.summary: Core empirical findings — rainfall intensity and duration thresholds that produce measurable road inundation in each study ward. Includes antecedent soil moisture correction factors and prior-day rainfall cumulative effects on inundation probability.

table_of_contents.title: Lake Backflow Contribution Analysis
table_of_contents.summary: Quantitative analysis of how lake fill levels amplify surface flood risk. Isolates lake backflow contribution from direct rainfall contribution for Bellandur, Varthur, and KR Puram lake systems. Provides compound probability curves for joint lake-fill and rainfall events.

table_of_contents.title: Road Corridor Vulnerability Rankings and Delay Multipliers
table_of_contents.summary: Empirical delay multipliers for six major Bangalore road corridors as a function of rainfall intensity band. Based on GPS probe vehicle data and incident records. Provides logistics planning reference for estimating travel time degradation under rain conditions.

table_of_contents.title: Early Warning Thresholds and Recommendations
table_of_contents.summary: Research-derived early warning threshold recommendations for each study ward. Covers lead time requirements, recommended monitoring parameters, and threshold values calibrated against observed flood events. Intended for integration into BBMP disaster management protocols.

result.title: BBMP Tree Canopy and Treefall Risk Reference — Bengaluru
result.agency: Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) — Horticulture Department
result.summary: BBMP tree census-derived reference document covering zone-level canopy density, ward-level tree risk ratings, fall-prone species identification, and high-risk treefall corridors mapped to road segments. Based on BBMP Tree Census data from November 2024. Primary reference for the Infrastructure Stress agent when assessing treefall risk and powerline proximity hazard.

table_of_contents.title: Zone-Level Canopy Density Summary
table_of_contents.summary: Zone-wise tree density and species composition across all 8 BBMP zones. Derived from the November 2024 tree census (approximately 592,000 trees enumerated). Covers total tree count, dominant species by zone, average tree age, and canopy coverage percentage per zone.

table_of_contents.title: Ward-Level Canopy Ratings and Powerline Proximity Risk
table_of_contents.summary: Ward-level assessment of tree canopy density and proximity to BESCOM overhead distribution lines. Each ward rated on canopy density, species mix, overhead line exposure, and resulting treefall-powerline contact risk. Covers all 16 wards.

table_of_contents.title: Fall-Prone Species Identification and Risk Profiles
table_of_contents.summary: Detailed risk profiles for the three primary fall-prone species in Bangalore — Rain Tree, Silver Oak, and Eucalyptus. Covers physical characteristics, failure modes, wind speed at which failure probability exceeds key thresholds, and geographic concentration by ward.

table_of_contents.title: High-Risk Treefall Corridors Mapped to Road Segments
table_of_contents.summary: Specific road segment level identification of high-risk treefall corridors where tree canopy, powerline proximity, and historical incident data combine to produce elevated treefall-outage risk. Provides actionable reference for infrastructure risk scoring and pre-storm crew positioning.

result.title: BESCOM Infrastructure and Storm Incident Reference — Bengaluru Distribution Network
result.summary: BESCOM distribution network reference covering HT/LT line inventory, historical storm-related outage analysis, feeder-level incident records, ward-level outage risk ratings, and storm response protocols. Primary reference for the Infrastructure Stress agent when reasoning about treefall-outage risk and feeder vulnerability during wind and rain events.

table_of_contents.title: Network Overview — HT/LT Lines, Substations, and Feeder Inventory
table_of_contents.summary: Complete inventory of BESCOM distribution network within BBMP limits. Covers HT and LT line totals, substation count and capacity, feeder count by zone, and network growth trends from 2018 to 2022. Establishes network scale context for risk analysis in subsequent sections.

table_of_contents.title: Treefall-Outage Correlation Analysis
table_of_contents.summary: Analysis of historical treefall-caused outage events from 2018–2022. Establishes wind gust thresholds at which treefall probability increases significantly for each dominant tree species. Identifies high-risk zone and species combinations. Primary reference for treefall risk scoring.

table_of_contents.title: Feeder-Level Incident History — Storm Events 2018–2022
table_of_contents.summary: Detailed incident record for each of the five primary monitored feeders. Covers total outage count, fault type distribution, wind conditions at time of event, affected customers, and average restoration time. Calibration data for feeder-level risk scoring.

table_of_contents.title: Ward-Level Outage Risk Ratings
table_of_contents.summary: Outage risk assessment for all 16 wards. Each ward rated on feeder exposure, overhead line density, proximity to high-canopy zones, historical incident rate, and critical load sensitivity. Provides overall outage risk tier and primary risk driver per ward.

table_of_contents.title: Storm Response Protocols and Restoration Time Benchmarks
table_of_contents.summary: BESCOM operational protocols for storm response including wind gust alert thresholds, crew pre-positioning guidelines, and restoration time benchmarks by fault type. Reference for estimating outage duration and customer impact in risk assessments.

result.title: BBMP Stormwater Drainage Master Plan — Bengaluru
result.summary: Comprehensive stormwater drainage master plan covering all BBMP ward boundaries. Documents existing drainage network capacity, identifies critical underpass and culvert choke points, establishes rainfall intensity thresholds for barricade deployment, and provides ward-level drainage capacity ratings. Primary reference for flood vulnerability assessment and underpass risk scoring.

table_of_contents.title: Overview and Scope of Bangalore Drainage Network
table_of_contents.summary: Background on Bangalore stormwater drainage network — total channel length, primary and secondary drain classifications, lake interconnection structure, and coverage gaps.

table_of_contents.title: ORR Corridor Culvert and Underpass Capacity Analysis
table_of_contents.summary: Hydraulic capacity analysis of all major underpasses and culverts on the Outer Ring Road corridor. Covers Bellandur, Koramangala, Silk Board, and BTM Layout underpasses. Includes design discharge values, observed failure thresholds, and barricade deployment trigger points.

table_of_contents.title: Sarjapur Road and Varthur Catchment Drainage Analysis
table_of_contents.summary: Catchment analysis for Sarjapur Road corridor and Varthur lake sub-basin. Documents drainage channel sizing, culvert conditions, lake outfall capacities, and road inundation risk. Varthur lake overflow behaviour and downstream impact on Sarjapur Road analysed in detail.

table_of_contents.title: North Bangalore Drainage — Hebbal, Yelahanka, Manyata Corridor
table_of_contents.summary: Drainage infrastructure assessment for north Bangalore including Hebbal lake system, Yelahanka tank cascade, and Manyata Tech Park catchment. Covers Bellary Road underpasses and Hebbal flyover drainage choke point.

table_of_contents.title: Critical Failure Zones, Threshold Summary, and Barricade Trigger Matrix
table_of_contents.summary: Consolidated reference table of all known drainage failure zones across BBMP limits. Specifies exact rainfall intensity thresholds (mm/hr) and lake fill percentages at which each underpass and road segment is recommended for barricading. Primary lookup table for BBMP disaster management teams.

table_of_contents.title: Ward-Level Drainage Capacity Ratings and Infrastructure Gaps
table_of_contents.summary: Summary ratings for all 16 BBMP wards. Each ward rated on primary drain coverage, secondary drain density, impervious surface ratio, and estimated drainage deficit. Identifies wards requiring priority investment.
